key_insight: Engineering the thin-film morphology of chiral 2D perovskite R-(MBA)2PbI4 makes lateral electrical transport measurable, revealing p-type conduction and strong morphology-dependent magnetotransport.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical route to integrate chiral perovskites into planar devices and opens the door to studying and exploiting CISS in spintronic architectures.
method: Morphology-engineered thin-film processing to reduce in-plane resistance
Hall-bar device fabrication for dark lateral transport measurements
Magnetotransport comparison across paths with and without grain boundaries
evidence_signal: Hall measurements in the dark showed p-type conduction with mobility of about 0.2 cm^2 V^-1 s^-1 and carrier density of about 3×10^14 cm^-2, with enhanced magnetoresistance when transport crossed grain boundaries.
watch_item: The measured mobility remains very low, so device performance may still be strongly limited by microstructure and grain-boundary variability.
paper_title: Morphology control and low-temperature magnetotransport in chiral 2D perovskite R-(MBA)$_2$PbI$_4$
